title: Salmon & Avocado Brown Rice Bowls
description: Flaky ponzu salmon over brown rice with marinated radishes and cucumber, seasoned avocado, and a spicy yogurt sauce. High protein, built around clean ingredients.

instructions.text: Cook the brown rice: combine rice with 4 cups water and a pinch of salt. Bring to a boil, reduce to low, cover, and cook 40–45 minutes until absorbed. Let steam 5 minutes off heat, then fluff and stir in mirin.

Make the soy glaze: combine soy sauce, honey, and rice vinegar in a small saucepan over medium heat. Bring to a simmer and cook 5–8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thickened and glossy. Remove from heat — it thickens more as it cools. You'll use about 4 tbsp total; refrigerate the rest (keeps 2 weeks).
Marinate the vegetables: combine sliced radishes and diced cucumbers in a bowl with 2 tbsp of the soy glaze. Stir to coat and set aside at least 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Season the avocado: dice avocado and toss with white wine vinegar and togarashi. Season with salt and pepper. Prep this close to serving — avocado doesn't hold well.
Make the spicy yogurt sauce: whisk together yogurt, mayo, sambal oelek, and 2 tbsp water until smooth. Season with salt and pepper. Adjust sambal oelek for heat.

Cook the salmon: pat fillets dry and season both sides with salt and pepper. Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium. Add salmon skin-side up — work in two batches if needed to avoid crowding. Loosely cover with foil and cook 3–5 minutes until lightly browned.

Flip salmon and cook skin-side down 2–4 minutes to 145°F internal. In the last 60 seconds, brush the flesh side with soy glaze. Transfer to a plate and repeat with remaining fillets if cooking in batches.
Remove and discard the skin. Flake salmon into large pieces with two forks. Add ponzu sauce and toss gently to coat.

Divide brown rice among four bowls. Top each with ponzu salmon, seasoned avocado, marinated vegetables with their liquid, and a drizzle of spicy yogurt sauce. Add a light drizzle of extra soy glaze if desired.

notes:
Store components separately for leftovers — rice and salmon reheat well, marinated vegetables improve overnight, spicy yogurt sauce keeps 3 days. Make fresh avocado when eating leftovers.

The soy glaze makes more than you need. Keep it — works on chicken thighs, roasted vegetables, or next week's salmon.

Two batches of 2 fillets is cleaner than crowding the pan. Crowding steams instead of browns.
